how can i dowloand this image by using developers tools

Mar 11

Here are a few easy ways to save/download the diagram:

Option 1: Right-click (Easiest)

  1. Right-click directly on the diagram in the preview
  2. Select "Save image as..." or "Copy image"

Option 2: Screenshot

  • Windows: Win + Shift + S to snip the area
  • Mac: Cmd + Shift + 4 to select and capture

Option 3: Developer Tools (as you asked)

  1. Press F12 (or Cmd + Option + I on Mac) to open DevTools
  2. Click the Console tab
  3. Paste this code and hit Enter:
javascript
const slide = document.querySelector('.slide');
const script = document.createElement('script');
script.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(script);
script.onload = () => {
  html2canvas(slide).then(canvas => {
    const a = document.createElement('a');
    a.download = 'industry-averages.png';
    a.href = canvas.toDataURL();
    a.click();
  });
};
  1. The image will automatically download as a PNG file

Option 3 is the cleanest as it captures exactly the slide element as a proper PNG. Let me know if you run into any issues!

I got this error: Unhandled Promise Rejection: Invalid element provided as first argument

Mar 11

The issue is that the script runs before the artifact iframe is focused. Try this instead — it targets the element more reliably:

Open DevTools Console (F12) and paste this:

javascript
const script = document.createElement('script');
script.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(script);
script.onload = () => {
  const slide = document.body;
  html2canvas(slide, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2
  }).then(canvas => {
    const a = document.createElement('a');
    a.download = 'industry-averages.png';
    a.href = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
    a.click();
  });
};

Important steps before running:

  1. Click inside the artifact/diagram preview first to focus it
  2. Then press F12 — this opens DevTools for the artifact's own iframe
  3. Make sure you're in the Console tab of that iframe's DevTools
  4. Then paste and run the code

Alternatively, the simplest fix is:

  1. Click the open in full page icon on the artifact (the expand/external link icon at the top right of the preview)
  2. Once it opens as a full page in your browser
  3. Open DevTools there with F12
  4. Paste the code in the Console

That way document.body will be exactly your diagram. Let me know how it goes!

SyntaxError: Can't create duplicate variable: 'slide'

Mar 11

The variable slide was already declared from the previous attempt. Just rename it to fix the conflict:

javascript
const script2 = document.createElement('script');
script2.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(script2);
script2.onload = () => {
  html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2
  }).then(canvas => {
    const a = document.createElement('a');
    a.download = 'industry-averages.png';
    a.href = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
    a.click();
  });
};

The key changes:

  • Renamed script → script2 to avoid the duplicate
  • Removed the slide variable entirely, using document.body directly instead

This should download cleanly! Let me know if it works.

it downloaded the screen not png

Mar 11

That's because DevTools was open on the main Claude page instead of the artifact iframe. Here's how to target the right frame:

Steps:

  1. Press F12 to open DevTools on the main Claude page
  2. At the top of the Console, you'll see a dropdown that says top
  3. Click that dropdown and select the iframe option (it will look something like artifact or a long URL)
  4. Now you're inside the artifact's context
  5. Paste and run the code:
javascript
const s = document.createElement('script');
s.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(s);
s.onload = () => {
  html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2
  }).then(canvas => {
    const a = document.createElement('a');
    a.download = 'industry-averages.png';
    a.href = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
    a.click();
  });
};

How to find the iframe dropdown:

  • In Chrome DevTools Console, look for a top ▼ dropdown in the top-left of the console panel
  • Click it and you'll see a list — pick the one that isn't top

That will run the code inside the diagram frame and download just the diagram. Let me know!

give me another script this was used already

Mar 11
2 / 2

Try this completely fresh script using a different approach with toBlob:

javascript
const sc = document.createElement('script');
sc.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(sc);
sc.onload = async () => {
  const canvas = await html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#f5f4f0'
  });
  canvas.toBlob(blob => {
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const link = document.createElement('a');
    link.href = url;
    link.download = 'diagram.png';
    document.body.appendChild(link);
    link.click();
    document.body.removeChild(link);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Make sure before running:

  • You switched the console context to the iframe (the top ▼ dropdown in DevTools Console)
  • You are not running it on the main Claude page

This uses completely new variable names (sc, blob, url, link) so no duplicate errors. Let me know!

worked thanks!

Mar 11

Glad it worked! 🎉

For future reference, the key steps were:

  1. Switch the console context from top to the artifact iframe using the dropdown
  2. Use fresh variable names each time to avoid duplicate errors

If you ever need to download another diagram, just repeat those steps with the last script. Let me know if you need anything else!

    lnk.download = 'diagram-clean.png';
    document.body.appendChild(lnk);
    lnk.click();
    document.body.removeChild(lnk);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(u);
  }, 'image/png');
};

please write me a script to download it from console

Mar 12

Here's a fresh script with all new variable names:

javascript
const scr = document.createElement('script');
scr.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(scr);
scr.onload = async () => {
  const cnv = await html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#f5f4f0'
  });
  cnv.toBlob(blb => {
    const obj = URL.createObjectURL(blb);
    const anc = document.createElement('a');
    anc.href = obj;
    anc.download = 'onboarding-process.png';
    document.body.appendChild(anc);
    anc.click();
    document.body.removeChild(anc);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(obj);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Remember the key steps:

  1. Press F12 to open DevTools
  2. Click the top ▼ dropdown in the Console
  3. Switch to the iframe context
  4. Paste and run the script

can u read it as trueloyaol onboarding steps and just?::

M1: Planning/PDR -  * This whole process should take no more than 3 weeks.  * We request your previous sales data, and Launch Preparation Questionnaire(LPQ). After receiving your previous sales data, we typically take 5-7 days to Analyze the data & create a Program Design Recommendation for you to look over and review, using both your data and LPQ. * We also look at building out workflow diagrams to ensure we have good alignment on how data will be flowing.  M2: Implementation -  * This process typically takes about 3-4 weeks. It can be done alongside M1 to make maximum use of time.  * Most integrations have documentation or plugins for ease of use. Those that do not, can be integrated through API/Webhooks for the same result. This typically takes the longest due to technical issues, whether its data not flowing as expected, or delays from technical teams.  * We are here to help answer any questions and guide your team through the integration process to ensure everything works properly. M3: UAT/Delivery -  * This process should take 2-3 weeks though can be extended if needed.  * This is a time for you to do your final testing of the program to ensure we have the correct information flowing, you can add members, complete activities, and redeem rewards.  * Once we confirm all issues are resolved and fully operational, it’s up to you to pick a date you want to ‘Launch’. When we deliver the full program to you, that is the point your onboarding journey is over, and you transition to our Customer Success team, where they will ensure your program stays healthy.

Mar 12
TrueLoyal Onboarding Process
Interactive artifact ∙ Version 2 

Here's the updated diagram using your actual onboarding content! It keeps the same colour palette and card style but now reflects the real process with:

  • Milestone 1 — Planning/PDR with the LPQ, data analysis, and workflow diagrams
  • Milestone 2 — Implementation with integration options and TrueLoyal support
  • Milestone 3 — UAT/Delivery through to Customer Success handoff

To download, use this fresh script in the iframe console:

javascript
const scx = document.createElement('script');
scx.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(scx);
scx.onload = async () => {
  const cv2 = await html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#f5f4f0'
  });
  cv2.toBlob(bl2 => {
    const ur2 = URL.createObjectURL(bl2);
    const an2 = document.createElement('a');
    an2.href = ur2;
    an2.download = 'onboarding-steps.png';
    document.body.appendChild(an2);
    an2.click();
    document.body.removeChild(an2);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(ur2);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Let me know if you'd like any content or design tweaks!

I don't wan a diagram for this I want ready to copy and paste into document

Mar 12

Here's the clean text ready to copy and paste:

TrueLoyal Onboarding Process

M1: Planning/PDR (Up to 3 weeks)

  • We request your previous sales data and Launch Preparation Questionnaire (LPQ) to get started. After receiving your data, we typically take 5–7 days to analyze it and create a Program Design Recommendation (PDR) for your review, using both your data and LPQ.
  • We also build out workflow diagrams to ensure strong alignment on how data will flow through the program.

M2: Implementation (3–4 weeks — can run alongside M1)

  • Most integrations have documentation or plugins for ease of use. Those that don't can be connected via API/Webhooks for the same result. This phase can take the longest due to technical factors, whether it's data not flowing as expected or delays from technical teams.
  • We are here to answer any questions and guide your team through the integration process to ensure everything works properly.

M3: UAT/Delivery (2–3 weeks, extendable if needed)

  • This is the time for your team to do final testing of the program — confirming correct data flow, adding members, completing activities, and redeeming rewards.
  • Once all issues are resolved and the program is fully operational, it's up to you to pick your Launch date. At the point of full delivery, your onboarding journey is complete and you transition to our Customer Success team, who will ensure your program stays healthy long-term.

javascript
const scz = document.createElement('script');
scz.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(scz);
scz.onload = async () => {
  const cvz = await html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#f5f4f0'
  });
  cvz.toBlob(blz => {
    const urz = URL.createObjectURL(blz);
    const anz = document.createElement('a');
    anz.href = urz;
    anz.download = 'team-diagram.png';
    document.body.appendChild(anz);
    anz.click();
    document.body.removeChild(anz);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(urz);
  }, 'image/png');
};

SyntaxError: Unexpected identifier 'Star'. Expected ')' to end an argument list.

Mar 16

That error means a previous variable is conflicting. Try this completely different approach using window to avoid any conflicts:

javascript
window._s = document.createElement('script');
window._s.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._s);
window._s.onload = async function() {
  var c = await html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#f5f4f0'
  });
  c.toBlob(function(b) {
    var u = URL.createObjectURL(b);
    var a = document.createElement('a');
    a.href = u;
    a.download = 'team-diagram.png';
    document.body.appendChild(a);
    a.click();
    document.body.removeChild(a);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(u);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Key differences:

  • Uses window._s instead of const to avoid duplicate variable errors
  • Uses var instead of const which is more forgiving in the console
  • Uses regular function() instead of arrow functions

Make sure you're in the iframe context (not top) before running! Let me know if it works.

TrueLoyal Team Diagram
Interactive artifact ∙ Version 2 

Done! Background is now white. Here's a fresh download script:

javascript
window._dl = document.createElement('script');
window._dl.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._dl);
window._dl.onload = async function() {
  var img = await html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#ffffff'
  });
  img.toBlob(function(blob) {
    var url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    var tag = document.createElement('a');
    tag.href = url;
    tag.download = 'team-diagram.png';
    document.body.appendChild(tag);
    tag.click();
    document.body.removeChild(tag);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Remember to switch to the iframe context in DevTools before running! 🎉

can u do the background transparent and give me code?

Mar 16
TrueLoyal Team Diagram
Interactive artifact ∙ Version 3 

Done! Here's the download script that preserves transparency:

javascript
window._tr = document.createElement('script');
window._tr.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._tr);
window._tr.onload = async function() {
  var img = await html2canvas(document.body, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: null
  });
  img.toBlob(function(blob) {
    var url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    var tag = document.createElement('a');
    tag.href = url;
    tag.download = 'team-diagram-transparent.png';
    document.body.appendChild(tag);
    tag.click();
    document.body.removeChild(tag);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  }, 'image/png');
};

The key is backgroundColor: null which tells html2canvas to keep the background transparent. Make sure to switch to the iframe context before running! 🎉

give me a code to downfalls diagram itself without background

Mar 16

Here's a script that targets just the diagram element, not the whole body:

javascript
window._dg = document.createElement('script');
window._dg.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._dg);
window._dg.onload = async function() {
  var el = document.querySelector('.wrapper');
  var rg = await html2canvas(el, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: null
  });
  rg.toBlob(function(bg) {
    var ug = URL.createObjectURL(bg);
    var lg = document.createElement('a');
    lg.href = ug;
    lg.download = 'team-diagram-only.png';
    document.body.appendChild(lg);
    lg.click();
    document.body.removeChild(lg);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(ug);
  }, 'image/png');
};

The key difference is document.querySelector('.wrapper') — this grabs only the diagram element itself, cutting out any background or padding around it. Switch to the iframe context first! 🎉

TrueLoyal Onboarding - Milestone Layout
Interactive artifact 

Here's the recreated diagram matching the style from your image, with the Milestone 1 Completed banner added at the bottom! To download, use this script in the iframe console:

javascript
window._mg = document.createElement('script');
window._mg.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._mg);
window._mg.onload = async function() {
  var em = document.querySelector('.page');
  var rm = await html2canvas(em, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: null
  });
  rm.toBlob(function(bm) {
    var um = URL.createObjectURL(bm);
    var lm = document.createElement('a');
    lm.href = um;
    lm.download = 'milestone1.png';
    document.body.appendChild(lm);
    lm.click();
    document.body.removeChild(lm);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(um);
  }, 'image/png');
};

window._t3.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._t3);
window._t3.onload = async function() {
  var et3 = document.querySelector('.page');
  var rt3 = await html2canvas(et3, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: null
  });
  rt3.toBlob(function(bt3) {
    var ut3 = URL.createObjectURL(bt3);
    var lt3 = document.createElement('a');
    lt3.href = ut3;
    lt3.download = 'milestone2.png';
    document.body.appendChild(lt3);
    lt3.click();
    document.body.removeChild(lt3);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(ut3);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Switch to the iframe context first then run! 🎉

window._m3.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._m3);
window._m3.onload = async function() {
  var em3 = document.querySelector('.page');

    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: null
  });
  rm3.toBlob(function(bm3) {
    var um3 = URL.createObjectURL(bm3);
    var lm3 = document.createElement('a');
    lm3.href = um3;
    lm3.download = 'milestone3.png';
    document.body.appendChild(lm3);
    lm3.click();
    document.body.removeChild(lm3);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(um3);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Unhandled Promise Rejection: Unable to find element in cloned iframe

Mar 16

Try this different approach using XMLSerializer to convert the SVG directly to an image, bypassing html2canvas entirely:

javascript
var svgEl = document.querySelector('svg');
var serializer = new XMLSerializer();
var svgStr = serializer.serializeToString(svgEl);
var svgBlob = new Blob([svgStr], {type: 'image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8'});
var svgUrl = URL.createObjectURL(svgBlob);
var imgEl = new Image();
imgEl.onload = function() {
  var canv = document.createElement('canvas');
  canv.width = svgEl.width.baseVal.value;
  canv.height = svgEl.height.baseVal.value;
  var ctx = canv.getContext('2d');
  ctx.drawImage(imgEl, 0, 0);
  canv.toBlob(function(blb) {
    var url = URL.createObjectURL(blb);
    var a = document.createElement('a');
    a.href = url;
    a.download = 'organic-shape.png';
    document.body.appendChild(a);
    a.click();
    document.body.removeChild(a);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  }, 'image/png');
  URL.revokeObjectURL(svgUrl);
};
imgEl.src = svgUrl;

This uses the browser's native SVG renderer instead of html2canvas — much more reliable for SVG elements. Switch to the iframe context first then run! 🎉

once again

Mar 16
javascript
var sv2 = document.querySelector('svg');
var sr2 = new XMLSerializer();
var ss2 = sr2.serializeToString(sv2);
var sb2 = new Blob([ss2], {type: 'image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8'});
var su2 = URL.createObjectURL(sb2);
var im2 = new Image();
im2.onload = function() {
  var ca2 = document.createElement('canvas');
  ca2.width = sv2.width.baseVal.value;
  ca2.height = sv2.height.baseVal.value;
  var cx2 = ca2.getContext('2d');
  cx2.drawImage(im2, 0, 0);
  ca2.toBlob(function(bl2) {
    var ur2 = URL.createObjectURL(bl2);
    var an2 = document.createElement('a');
    an2.href = ur2;
    an2.download = 'organic-shape.png';
    document.body.appendChild(an2);
    an2.click();
    document.body.removeChild(an2);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(ur2);
  }, 'image/png');
  URL.revokeObjectURL(su2);
};
im2.src = su2;

All brand new variable names — switch to iframe context first then run! 🎉

do one more code this is not working

Mar 16

Try this completely different approach using fetch and createObjectURL:

javascript
var el3 = document.querySelector('svg');
var xm3 = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(el3);
var en3 = btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(xm3)));
var ur3 = 'data:image/svg+xml;base64,' + en3;
var im3 = new Image();
im3.onload = function() {
  var cv3 = document.createElement('canvas');
  cv3.width = 2080;
  cv3.height = 880;
  var ct3 = cv3.getContext('2d');
  ct3.drawImage(im3, 0, 0, 2080, 880);
  var lk3 = document.createElement('a');
  lk3.download = 'organic-shape.png';
  lk3.href = cv3.toDataURL('image/png');
  lk3.click();
};
im3.src = ur3;

This uses base64 encoding instead of createObjectURL — a completely different method. Switch to iframe context first then run! 🎉

script error

Mar 16

Let's try the simplest possible approach — just download it as an SVG file instead of PNG:

javascript
var el4 = document.querySelector('svg');
var xm4 = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(el4);
var bl4 = new Blob([xm4], {type: 'image/svg+xml'});
var ur4 = URL.createObjectURL(bl4);
var lk4 = document.createElement('a');
lk4.href = ur4;
lk4.download = 'organic-shape.svg';
document.body.appendChild(lk4);
lk4.click();
document.body.removeChild(lk4);
URL.revokeObjectURL(ur4);

This downloads it as an SVG file which:

  • Is lossless and scalable to any size
  • Can be opened in any browser
  • Can be imported into PowerPoint, Figma, Illustrator etc.
  • Can be converted to PNG easily online at svgtopng.com

Switch to iframe context first then run! 🎉

The canvas element isn't found. Try this — it creates its own canvas on the fly:

javascript
var svg6 = document.getElementById('mysvg');
var dat6 = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(svg6);
var enc6 = 'data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,' + encodeURIComponent(dat6);
var can6 = document.createElement('canvas');
can6.width = 2080;
can6.height = 880;
var ctx6 = can6.getContext('2d');
var img6 = new Image();
img6.onload = function() {
  ctx6.drawImage(img6, 0, 0, 2080, 880);
  var lnk6 = document.createElement('a');
  lnk6.download = 'organic-shape.png';
  lnk6.href = can6.toDataURL('image/png');
  lnk6.click();
};
img6.src = enc6;

No reliance on existing elements — creates everything fresh! Switch to iframe context first then run! 🎉

  lnk8.download = 'organic-shape.png';

Refused to load data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAACCAAAANwCAYAAAD034nbAAAAAXNSR0IArs4c6QAAAERlWElmTU0AKgAAAAgAAYdpAAQAAAABAAAAGgAAAAAAA6ABAAMAAAABAAEAAKACAAQAAAABAAAIIKADAAQAAAABAAADcAAAAAAFUHGaAABAAElEQVR4AezdCbxd110f+rX3uaPupHm4upZl2SEBB0IaQprEg0ic0DQM7afk9VOgvADpSIfHo3SgpfhBW4by4AF9QB9QwhCGJFBCoSGDjWw5cVLikIQ44MR2ZFlX1jzcQbrDOXu//z5XcmRjx1fSHc7w3cnyHs4e1vruqzuc/TtrpWQiQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIECBAgQIAAAQIEC...EEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBBBAAAEEEEAAAQQQQAABBK5O4P8DFu0lnhwnU/QAAAAASUVORK5CYII= because it does not appear in the frame-src directive of the Content Security Policy.

Mar 16

The browser is blocking the download due to security policy. Let me add a download button inside the artifact that uses a different method:

Organic Shape
Interactive artifact ∙ Version 8 

The Content Security Policy blocks PNG downloads from the console. Instead:

  1. Click ⬇ Download as SVG button in the preview
  2. Then go to svgtopng.com to convert it to PNG in seconds

SVG is actually better quality since it's fully scalable! 🎉

can u give me a code to png

Mar 16

The CSP (Content Security Policy) on claude.ai is blocking PNG downloads from within the artifact. The only reliable way is:

  1. Click the expand icon (⤢) on the top right of the artifact to open it as a full page
  2. Then in that full page, open DevTools F12
  3. Make sure you're in top context (not iframe this time)
  4. Run this:
javascript
var svg9 = document.querySelector('svg');
var dat9 = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(svg9);
var enc9 = 'data:image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8,' + encodeURIComponent(dat9);
var can9 = document.createElement('canvas');
can9.width = 2080;
can9.height = 880;
var ctx9 = can9.getContext('2d');
var img9 = new Image();
img9.onload = function() {
  ctx9.drawImage(img9, 0, 0, 2080, 880);
  var lnk9 = document.createElement('a');
  lnk9.download = 'organic-shape.png';
  lnk9.href = can9.toDataURL('image/png');
  lnk9.click();
};
img9.src = enc9;

Opening it as a full page removes the CSP restriction that was blocking it! 🎉

Done! Here's the download code — run it in the iframe context:

javascript
var sva = document.getElementById('mysvg');
var dta = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(sva);
var bla = new Blob([dta], {type: 'image/svg+xml'});
var ura = URL.createObjectURL(bla);

I want it png

Mar 16

The only reliable way to get it as PNG from claude.ai is:

  1. Click the ⤢ expand icon on the top right of the artifact to open it as a full page
  2. Once open in full page, right-click on the shape
  3. Select "Save image as..."

That will save it directly as PNG — no code needed and no CSP restrictions! 🎉

I want a code to download it in png with transparent backgroumd

Mar 16

Try this — open the artifact in full page first (click ⤢), then run in console:

javascript
var fg = document.getElementById('mysvg');
var ww = fg.getBoundingClientRect().width;
var hh = fg.getBoundingClientRect().height;
var cf = document.createElement('canvas');
cf.width = ww;
cf.height = hh;
var xf = cf.getContext('2d');
var df = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(fg);
var uf = 'data:image/svg+xml;base64,' + btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(df)));
var mf = new Image();
mf.onload = function() {
  xf.clearRect(0, 0, ww, hh);
  xf.drawImage(mf, 0, 0, ww, hh);
  var tf = document.createElement('a');
  tf.download = 'organic-shape.png';
  tf.href = cf.toDataURL('image/png');
  document.body.appendChild(tf);
  tf.click();
  document.body.removeChild(tf);
};
mf.src = uf;

Important: Open the artifact as a full page (⤢ icon) first, then run in top context — not iframe! 🎉

Mar 16

Mar 16

kontynuuj

Mar 16

Jasne! Wróćmy do roadmapy. 😊

Potrzebuję treści Twojego procesu onboardingu klienta — możesz:

  • Wkleić tekst z opisem kroków
  • Wgrać zdjęcie dokumentu lub slajdu
  • Opisać mi etapy własnymi słowami

Co masz przygotowane?

co oznacza ten error:

WordPress database error You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MariaDB server version for the right syntax to use near ''zinrelo_cart' WHERE cart_timeout < '2026-03-04 09:42:55' AND cron_status = '0'' at line 1 for query SELECT id, zinrelo_transaction_id FROM wp_.'zinrelo_cart' WHERE cart_timeout < '2026-03-04 09:42:55' AND cron_status = '0' made by do_action_ref_array('zinrelo_every_five_minute_event'), WP_Hook->do_action, WP_Hook->apply_filters, zinrelo_every_five_minute_cronjob, zinrelo_update_status

Mar 17

To jest błąd SQL w pluginie Zinrelo (plugin lojalnościowy) w WordPress. Problem jest w zapytaniu do bazy danych.

Co oznacza ten błąd:

Zapytanie SQL ma nieprawidłową składnię — konkretnie tutaj:

sql
FROM wp_.'zinrelo_cart'

Zamiast poprawnego:

sql
FROM wp_zinrelo_cart

Widać że nazwa tabeli ma zbędne znaki — apostrof ' i kropkę . przed nazwą tabeli, co powoduje błąd składni SQL.

Możliwe przyczyny:

  • Bug w kodzie pluginu Zinrelo
  • Nieprawidłowy prefiks tabeli w konfiguracji
  • Uszkodzona instalacja pluginu
  • Niekompatybilna wersja pluginu z aktualną wersją MariaDB/MySQL

Co zrobić:

  1. Zaktualizuj plugin Zinrelo do najnowszej wersji — to najprostsze rozwiązanie
  2. Jeśli już jest aktualny, dezaktywuj i aktywuj ponownie plugin
  3. Sprawdź w wp-config.php czy prefiks tabeli $table_prefix jest ustawiony poprawnie (domyślnie wp_)
  4. Jeśli problem nie zniknie — skontaktuj się z supportem Zinrelo i wyślij im ten błąd

To nie jest krytyczny błąd bezpieczeństwa, ale może powodować że niektóre funkcje pluginu nie działają poprawnie. Daj znać jeśli potrzebujesz więcej pomocy! 😊

woocoommerce wtyczka nie chce edzialac na wordpresie https://woocommerce.com/auto-install/step/install/18734003407318/?wccom-install-nonce=2cfd1f9862&stop-on-success=0&site-url=https%3A%2F%2Ftest085stg.wpenginepowered.com%2F&success-url=https%3A%2F%2Ftest085stg.wpenginepowered.com%2Fwp-admin%2Fadmin.php%3Fpage%3Dwc-admin%26path%3D%2Fextensions%26tab%3Dmy-subscriptions

Mar 17

Widzę że wklejasz link do automatycznej instalacji WooCommerce — nie mogę go otworzyć bo zawiera prywatne tokeny instalacyjne.

Powiedz mi więcej o problemie:

  • Jaki błąd widzisz podczas instalacji?
  • Czy WooCommerce jest już zainstalowany ale nie działa, czy nie można go zainstalować?
  • Czy widzisz jakiś komunikat błędu na ekranie?

Najczęstsze przyczyny problemów z WooCommerce na WordPress:

  1. Za stara wersja PHP — WooCommerce wymaga PHP 7.4 lub wyżej (najlepiej 8.0+)
  2. Za mało pamięci RAM — potrzeba minimum 128MB, najlepiej 256MB
  3. Konflikt z innym pluginem — spróbuj dezaktywować inne pluginy
  4. Uprawnienia plików — błędne uprawnienia folderów na serwerze
  5. WP Engine blokuje instalację — hosting WP Engine ma własne ograniczenia bezpieczeństwa

Szybkie rozwiązanie na WP Engine:

  • Zainstaluj WooCommerce ręcznie — pobierz ZIP z wordpress.org i wgraj przez Wtyczki → Dodaj nową → Wyślij wtyczkę

Jaki dokładnie błąd widzisz? 😊

dostaje takie powiadomienie

Mar 17

To jest tylko ostrzeżenie (nie krytyczny błąd) — WooCommerce informuje że niektóre aktywne wtyczki są niekompatybilne z aktualnymi funkcjami WooCommerce.

Co zrobić:

  1. Kliknij "review the details" w tym komunikacie
  2. Zobaczysz listę wtyczek które powodują konflikt
  3. Wyślij mi screenshot tej listy — pomogę Ci zidentyfikować problem 😊

Najczęściej konfliktują z WooCommerce:

  • Stare wtyczki cache (W3 Total Cache, WP Super Cache)
  • Stare wtyczki SEO
  • Zinrelo (który już widziałeś wcześniej z błędem SQL!)
  • Wtyczki page builderów (Elementor, Divi)

Kliknij "review the details" i pokaż mi co tam jest! 😊

Mar 17

Tak jak się spodziewałem — to Zinrelo powoduje konflikt! Ten sam plugin który miał błąd SQL wcześniej 😊

Masz dwie opcje:

Opcja 1: Zostaw Zinrelo aktywny (jeśli go potrzebujesz)

  • To tylko ostrzeżenie, WooCommerce nadal będzie działać
  • Skontaktuj się z supportem Zinrelo i poproś o aktualizację kompatybilną z WooCommerce
  • Zinrelo wersja 1.0.1 jest bardzo stara — sprawdź czy jest nowsza wersja

Opcja 2: Dezaktywuj Zinrelo tymczasowo

  • Kliknij "Deactivate" pod Zinrelo
  • Sprawdź czy WooCommerce działa poprawnie
  • Reaktywuj Zinrelo gdy będzie zaktualizowany

Moje zalecenie: Skoro używasz TrueLoyal (widzę z poprzednich diagramów) jako platformy lojalnościowej, zapytaj zespół TrueLoyal czy Zinrelo jest nadal potrzebny i czy jest kompatybilna wersja z WooCommerce. 😊

Czy WooCommerce działa pomimo tego ostrzeżenia?

can u recreate this diagram exactly like it is but with this color patella as used before?

Mar 18
Repeat Revenue Dashboard
Interactive artifact 

Here's the dashboard recreated in the TrueLoyal colour palette — navy, pink, and cream! It includes:

  • Line chart with navy for the evaluation period and pink for projected
  • Slider for the evaluation period days
  • All metric cards with the same values from your original
  • Right panel with the $38.87K highlight, progress bar, and description

Would you like any tweaks to the layout or data? 😊

can u Gove me code to download from console as png

Mar 18
javascript
window._dc = document.createElement('script');
window._dc.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._dc);
window._dc.onload = async function() {
  var edc = document.querySelector('body');
  var rdc = await html2canvas(edc, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#f5f4f0'
  });
  rdc.toBlob(function(bdc) {
    var udc = URL.createObjectURL(bdc);
    var ldc = document.createElement('a');
    ldc.href = udc;
    ldc.download = 'revenue-dashboard.png';
    document.body.appendChild(ldc);
    ldc.click();
    document.body.removeChild(ldc);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(udc);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Switch to the iframe context first (top ▼ dropdown in DevTools console) then run! 🎉

TypeError: null is not an object (evaluating 'fg.getBoundingClientRect')

Mar 18

That error is from an old script still in memory. Try this fresh one:

javascript
window._dd = document.createElement('script');
window._dd.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._dd);
window._dd.onload = async function() {
  var tgt = document.getElementsByTagName('body')[0];
  var res = await html2canvas(tgt, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#f5f4f0',
    logging: false,
    allowTaint: true
  });
  res.toBlob(function(blob) {
    var url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    var a = document.createElement('a');
    a.href = url;
    a.download = 'revenue-dashboard.png';
    document.body.appendChild(a);
    a.click();
    document.body.removeChild(a);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  }, 'image/png');
};

Make sure you're in the iframe context (not top) before running! 🎉

hey, I wan to build roadmap/timeline for the projects and statuses of completion and dates and overdue which are managed on Monday.com how can I do it not only on Monday but also on external platforms maybe?

Mar 19

Great idea! Here are your main options:

Staying within Monday.com:

  • Monday has a built-in Timeline/Gantt view — you can add dates, statuses, dependencies and colour-code by status (on track, overdue, done)
  • Use Dashboard widgets to show progress across multiple boards
  • Monday also has a Workload view to see who is overloaded

Connecting Monday to external platforms:

1. Notion

  • Sync Monday items to Notion via Zapier or Make (formerly Integromat)
  • Build a visual roadmap in Notion's Timeline view
  • Good for sharing with clients who don't have Monday access

2. Miro / FigJam

  • Connect via Monday's integration marketplace
  • Great for visual roadmaps you can present in meetings
  • More creative/flexible layout than Monday's native views

3. PowerPoint / Google Slides

  • Export Monday data and build presentation-ready roadmaps
  • Tools like Piktochart or Beautiful.ai can auto-generate slides from data

4. Airtable

  • Sync data from Monday via Zapier
  • Has powerful Gantt and timeline views
  • Better for complex filtering and reporting

5. Custom built (what I can help with!)

  • I can build you a live interactive roadmap that pulls data directly from your Monday.com boards using the Monday API
  • Would show real-time statuses, dates, overdue items — all in your TrueLoyal colour palette
  • Embeddable anywhere

Which direction interests you most? And specifically:

What are you thinking? I can start building right away once I know the direction! 😊

Q: What would you like to build? A: Monday + external sync setup

Q: Who needs to view the roadmap? (Select all that apply) A: Internal team only

Mar 19

Perfect! Here's a practical setup to sync Monday.com with an external platform for your internal team:

Best combo for internal teams: Monday.com + Notion

This is the most popular setup because it's easy to maintain and looks great.

How to set it up:

Step 1 — Set up Monday.com correctly

  • Make sure each project has: Start date, End date, Status, Owner, Priority
  • Use Monday's Timeline view as your source of truth
  • Create a dedicated "Master Roadmap" board that pulls items from all project boards using Monday's Mirror columns

Step 2 — Connect to Notion via Make (Integromat)

  • Create a free account at make.com
  • Build a scenario: Monday.com → Notion
  • Trigger: when item status changes in Monday
  • Action: update corresponding page in Notion
  • This keeps both platforms in sync automatically

Step 3 — Build the Notion Roadmap

  • Create a Notion database with Timeline view
  • Map fields: Name, Status, Dates, Owner, Priority
  • Colour code by status — On Track (green), At Risk (amber), Overdue (red), Done (navy)

Alternative: Monday + Slack notifications

  • Set up Monday automations to post to a Slack channel when:
    • A task becomes overdue
    • A milestone is completed
    • Status changes to "At Risk"

Or — I can build you a custom roadmap artifact that connects directly to your Monday.com boards via the API and displays everything visually. Since you're already connected to Monday.com here in Claude, I can build it right now!

Q: Which would you like to start with? A: Build custom roadmap using Monday API

Mar 19

Great choice! Since you're already connected to Monday.com, let me first fetch your boards to see what data we're working with.

Connector search
1 connector
monday.com
Connectors that could help
monday.com
Manage projects, boards, and workflows in monday.com
Looking for something else? Browse all connectors

It looks like your Monday.com connector needs to be enabled first. Once you connect it I can:

  • Fetch all your boards and projects automatically
  • Pull statuses, dates, owners and priorities
  • Build a live interactive roadmap in your TrueLoyal colour palette showing overdue, on track and completed items

To connect:

  1. Click the Connect button above
  2. Authorise Monday.com access
  3. Come back here and I'll immediately pull your boards and build the roadmap! 😊

nono i exported it to exel and want to do power point how to do so

Mar 19

Got it! Here's how to turn your Monday.com Excel export into a PowerPoint roadmap:

Option 1: Manual in PowerPoint (Most control)

  1. Open your Excel export from Monday
  2. Open PowerPoint and pick a blank slide
  3. Go to Insert → SmartArt → Process and pick a timeline style
  4. Or use Insert → Table to build a Gantt-style grid
  5. Colour code rows by status manually

Option 2: Use PowerPoint's built-in Timeline (Easiest)

  1. Go to Insert → SmartArt
  2. Choose Process → Basic Timeline or Circle Accent Timeline
  3. Type in your project names and dates
  4. Change colours to match your brand

Option 3: Let me build it for you! (Best result)

Upload your Excel file here and I will:

  • Read all your project data automatically
  • Build a beautiful interactive roadmap in your TrueLoyal colour palette
  • Include statuses, dates, overdue flags
  • Give you a version you can screenshot/download for PowerPoint

Q: Which option would you like? A: Upload Excel — build it for me

Mar 19

Perfect! Go ahead and upload your Excel file exported from Monday.com and I'll build the roadmap right away in your TrueLoyal colour palette! 😊

Just drag and drop the file into the chat or click the attachment icon.

I cannot upload xlxs

Mar 19

No problem! You can share the data in a few other ways:

Option 1: Copy & Paste

  • Open your Excel file
  • Select all the data (Ctrl+A)
  • Copy (Ctrl+C)
  • Paste it directly into the chat

Option 2: Save as CSV

  • In Excel go to File → Save As
  • Choose CSV (Comma delimited) format
  • Then upload the CSV file here — CSV uploads are supported!

Option 3: Tell me the columns

  • Just tell me what columns you have in your export (e.g. Project Name, Status, Start Date, End Date, Owner etc.)
  • Give me a few rows of sample data
  • I'll build the roadmap based on that

Which works best for you? 😊

pdf

Mar 19

Perfect, I have all the data! Let me build the roadmap now.

Onboarding Roadmap 2026
Interactive artifact 

Here's your full onboarding roadmap! It includes:

  • All 3 sections — Upcoming, Active, and Launched
  • Filter buttons — click to filter by Overdue, M1, M2, M3, On Track
  • Colour coded badges for each phase and status
  • Days left or overdue for every project

Key highlights from your data:

  • 17 projects are overdue — Brook Mays Music (290 days!) and Colibri-McKissock (290 days) are the most critical
  • 6 projects are on track
  • 1 project is paused (Office Depot)

To download use the same console script from before! 😊 Want me to add a Gantt chart view as well?

can u give me this code to download

Mar 19
javascript
window._rp = document.createElement('script');
window._rp.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/html2canvas/1.4.1/html2canvas.min.js';
document.head.appendChild(window._rp);
window._rp.onload = async function() {
  var erp = document.getElementsByTagName('body')[0];
  var rrp = await html2canvas(erp, {
    useCORS: true,
    scale: 2,
    backgroundColor: '#f5f4f0',
    logging: false,
    allowTaint: true,
    scrollY: 0,
    windowHeight: document.body.scrollHeight,
    height: document.body.scrollHeight
  });
  rrp.toBlob(function(brp) {
    var urp = URL.createObjectURL(brp);
    var lrp = document.createElement('a');
    lrp.href = urp;
    lrp.download = 'onboarding-roadmap.png';
    document.body.appendChild(lrp);
    lrp.click();
    document.body.removeChild(lrp);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(urp);
  }, 'image/png');
};

This captures the full page height so nothing gets cut off. Switch to iframe context first then run! 🎉

but it is as png it is not clicable

Mar 19

You're right! PNG is just an image — it loses all the interactivity. Here are your options:

Option 1: Download as HTML file (keeps filtering/clicking) Run this in the iframe console:

javascript
var blob = new Blob([document.documentElement.outerHTML], {type: 'text/html'});
var url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
var a = document.createElement('a');
a.href = url;
a.download = 'onboarding-roadmap.html';
document.body.appendChild(a);
a.click();
document.body.removeChild(a);
URL.revokeObjectURL(url);

Then open the downloaded HTML file in any browser — fully interactive! ✅

Option 2: For PowerPoint Since you mentioned PowerPoint earlier, PNG is actually the right format for that. You'd paste it as an image on a slide.

Option 3: Host it online Upload the HTML file to a free hosting service like:

  • Netlify Drop — drag & drop at netlify.com/drop
  • GitHub Pages — free hosting
  • Share the link with your team — fully clickable for everyone!

[User denied question request]

Mar 19

No problem! Here's the HTML download code — run it in the iframe context:

javascript
var bh = new Blob([document.documentElement.outerHTML], {type: 'text/html'});
var uh = URL.createObjectURL(bh);
var ah = document.createElement('a');
ah.href = uh;
ah.download = 'onboarding-roadmap.html';
document.body.appendChild(ah);
ah.click();
document.body.removeChild(ah);
URL.revokeObjectURL(uh);

Once downloaded, just open the file in any browser and it will be fully interactive with all the filter buttons working! 🎉

ok how I can put it in GitHub pages

Mar 19

Here's a step by step guide:

Step 1: Create a GitHub account

  • Go to github.com and sign up (free)

Step 2: Create a new repository

  • Click the + icon top right → New repository
  • Name it something like onboarding-roadmap
  • Set it to Public
  • Check Add a README file
  • Click Create repository

Step 3: Upload your HTML file

  • Inside your new repo click Add file → Upload files
  • Drag and drop your onboarding-roadmap.html file
  • Rename it to index.html before uploading — this is important!
  • Click Commit changes

Step 4: Enable GitHub Pages

  • Go to Settings tab in your repo
  • Scroll down to Pages in the left sidebar
  • Under Source select Deploy from a branch
  • Select main branch → / (root)
  • Click Save

Step 5: Get your link

  • Wait 1-2 minutes
  • Your site will be live at: https://yourusername.github.io/onboarding-roadmap
  • Share this link with your team!

Tips:

  • Every time you update the HTML file and upload it, the site automatically updates
  • You can keep it private by making the repo private (requires GitHub Pro)

Want me to help with anything else? 😊

is this link visible for anyone who opens it? file:///Users/Beata%20Rusin-Siwiecka/Downloads/onboarding-roadmap.html

Mar 19

No! That link is only visible to you on your own computer.

The file:/// at the beginning means it's a local file stored on your Mac — nobody else can access it.

To share it you have 3 options:

Option 1: GitHub Pages (as we discussed)

  • Gives you a public https:// link
  • Anyone with the link can open it

Option 2: Send the HTML file directly

  • Email the onboarding-roadmap.html file as an attachment
  • The recipient downloads it and opens in their browser
  • Works offline, no hosting needed

Option 3: Netlify Drop (easiest — 30 seconds!)

  1. Go to netlify.com/drop
  2. Drag and drop your onboarding-roadmap.html file
  3. Instantly get a public link like https://random-name.netlify.app
  4. Share with anyone!

Netlify Drop is probably the fastest option if you just want a quick shareable link without setting up GitHub. Would you like to try that? 😊

I just uploaded it into GitHub how can I open the link and share it

Mar 19

Great! Here's how to get your live link:

Step 1: Enable GitHub Pages

  1. Go to your repository on github.com
  2. Click Settings tab (top menu)
  3. In the left sidebar scroll down to Pages
  4. Under Source select Deploy from a branch
  5. Select main → / (root)
  6. Click Save

Step 2: Get your link

  • Wait 1-2 minutes
  • Refresh the Pages settings page
  • You'll see a green box saying "Your site is live at:"
  • The link will look like: https://yourusername.github.io/onboarding-roadmap

Step 3: Share it

  • Copy that link and send it to anyone
  • They can open it in any browser — no login needed!

Quick check — did you:

  • Name the file index.html when uploading?
  • Set the repo to Public?

If the file is still named onboarding-roadmap.html the link won't work automatically. Let me know what you see and I'll help! 😊

No problem! The easiest ways without Monday access:

Option 1: Keep exporting PDF/Excel from Monday

  • Export from Monday whenever you need to update
  • Drop the file here and I'll update the roadmap instantly

Option 2: Your GitHub Pages link

Monday.com export (weekly)
        ↓
Paste/upload here to Claude
        ↓
I update the roadmap HTML
        ↓
You upload to GitHub Pages
        ↓
Team opens the same link — always up to date!

This way you have a live shareable link that updates weekly with minimal effort.

how can I pin this conversation

Mar 20

Unfortunately Claude doesn't have a built-in pin feature yet, but here are some workarounds:

Option 1: Bookmark it in your browser

  • Copy the URL of this conversation from your browser address bar
  • Press Ctrl+D (Windows) or Cmd+D (Mac) to bookmark it
  • Name it something like "TrueLoyal Roadmap"

Option 2: Star/favourite in Claude

  • On the left sidebar in Claude, hover over this conversation
  • Click the three dots (...) that appear
  • See if there's a Star or Favourite option

Option 3: Rename the conversation

  • Hover over the conversation in the left sidebar
  • Click the three dots (...)
  • Select Rename
  • Name it something memorable like "TrueLoyal Roadmap & Diagrams"
  • It will be easy to find by searching

Option 4: Save the link

  • Copy the conversation URL
  • Save it in a Notion page, Monday.com item, or Slack message to yourself

The bookmark + rename combo is probably the quickest! That way you can always find it in your browser bookmarks and also spot it easily in the Claude sidebar 😊

11:51 AM
Onboarding Roadmap 2026 - Updated
Interactive artifact ∙ Version 2 

Updated! Here's what changed from last week:

  • Megabus 🆕 added as a new M1 project (Beata's first solo project!)
  • Kroll International moved from M1 → M2
  • Starkford is now overdue (was on track last week)
  • Progress bars added for every project
  • Summary cards at the top showing totals at a glance

To download as HTML run this in the iframe console:

javascript
var bh2 = new Blob([document.documentElement.outerHTML], {type: 'text/html'});
var uh2 = URL.createObjectURL(bh2);
var ah2 = document.createElement('a');
ah2.href = uh2;
ah2.download = 'onboarding-roadmap-march2026.html';
document.body.appendChild(ah2);
ah2.click();
document.body.removeChild(ah2);
URL.revokeObjectURL(uh2);

Then upload to GitHub Pages to update your team's link! 🎉
